title: Compositions and methods of use for reducing evaporative loss from swimming pools and spas
abstract: The present invention relates to microbial compositions comprising bacteria selected from the genus Bacillus useful in treating swimming pool water to reduce water evaporation.
claims:
Claims We Claim: 1. A method of treating water in a swimming pool comprising contacting the water with a composition comprising a mixture of bacteria selected from the genus Bacillus. 2. The method of claim 1, wherein each of the Bacillus in the mixture is individually aerobically fermented, harvested, dried, and ground to produce a mixture having a mean particle size of about 200 microns, with greater than about 60% of the powder in the size range between 100 - 800 microns. 3. The method of claim 1 or 2, wherein the mixture of bacteria is selected solely from the genus Bacillus. 4. The method of any one of the preceding claims, wherein the composition comprises one to seven different Bacillus strains. 5. The method of any one of the preceding claims, the Bacillus is Bacillus subtilis, Bacillus amyloliquefaciens, Bacillus licheniformis, Bacillus pumilus, Bacillus megaterium, Bacillus coagulans, or Paenibacillus polymyxa. 6. The method of claim 5, wherein the Bacillus subtilis is Bacillus mojavensis, Bacillus subtilis 34KLB, or a combination thereof. 7. The method of any one of the preceding claims, wherein the composition comprises Bacillus subtilis, Bacillus amyloliquefaciens, Bacillus licheniformis and Bacillus pumilus. 8. The method of any one of claims 1-2 and 4-7, wherein the composition further comprises Pediococcus acidilactici, Pediococcus pentosaceus, and Lactobacillus plantar um. 9. The method of any one of the preceding claims, wherein the composition further comprises an inert carrier. 10. The method of claim 9, wherein the inert carrier is anhydrous dextrose, dextrose monohydrate, dendritic salt or a mixture thereof. 11. The method of any one of the preceding claims, wherein the composition further comprises from 0.1 to 10% by weight of a supplemental fat, oil, or grease component. 12. The method of claim 1 1 , wherein the supplemental fat, oil, or grease component is triolein, tristearin, tripalmitin, soy lecithin, olive oil, almond oil, peanut oil, sunflower oil, rapeseed oil, soybean oil, linseed oil, corn oil, cod liver oil, palm oil, coconut oil, cacao butter, or various esters of lauric, myristic, oleic, palmitoleic, linoleic or arachinonic acids, and mixtures thereof, with glycerol, methanol, ethanol, propanol or isopropanol. 13. The method of claim 10, wherein the inert carrier is dextrose monohydrate. 14. The method of any one of the preceding claims, wherein the composition has a moisture content of less than about 5%; and a final bacterial concentration of about 10 5 - 10 11 colony forming units (CFU) per gram of the composition. 15. The method of any one of claims 9-14, wherein the inert carrier is at a concentration of about between 75 - 99.9% (w/w). 16. The method of any one of the preceding claims, wherein the water is contacted by contacting a swimming pool filtration unit with the composition. 17. The method of any one of the preceding claims, wherein the composition is delivered directly into the swimming pool water. 18. The method of any one of the preceding claims, wherein the composition is contained in a dosing device that is added to the pool and allows the composition to release over a period of time. 19. The method of any one of the preceding claims, wherein treating the water in swimming pools results in increased clarity of the water, decreased fats, oils, and grease (FOG) concentrations, reduced water evaporation, better heat retention, reduced head pressure at the filter, reduced pump run time and/or energy savings. 20. A composition for treating water in recreational waters, comprising a mixture of bacteria selected solely from the genus Bacillus wherein each of the Bacillus in the mixture is individually aerobically fermented, harvested, dried, and ground to produce a mixture having a mean particle size of about 200 microns, with greater than about 60% of the powder in the size range between 100 - 800 microns and an inert carrier. 21. The composition of claim 20, comprising one to seven different Bacillus strains. 22. The composition of claim 20 or 21 , wherein the Bacillus is Bacillus subtilis, Bacillus amyloliquefaciens , Bacillus licheniformis, Bacillus pumilus, Bacillus megaterium, Bacillus coagulans, or Paenibacillus polymyxa. 23. The composition of any one of claims 20-22, wherein the inert carrier is anhydrous dextrose, dextrose monohydrate, dendritic salt or a mixture thereof. 24. The composition of any one of claims 20-23, further comprising from 0.1 to 10% by weight of a supplemental fat, oil, or grease component. 25. The composition of claim 24, wherein the supplemental fat, oil, or grease component is triolein, tristearin, tripalmitin, soy lecithin, olive oil, almond oil, peanut oil, sunflower oil, rapeseed oil, soybean oil, linseed oil, com oil, cod liver oil, palm oil, coconut oil, cacao butter, or various esters of lauric, myristic, oleic, palmitoleic, linoleic or 

 arachinonic acids, and mixtures thereof, with glycerol, methanol, ethanol, propanol or isopropanol. 26. The composition of any one of claims 20-25, wherein the composition comprises Bacillus subtilis, Bacillus amyloliquefaciens, Bacillus licheniformis and Bacillus pumilus. 27. The composition of any one of claims 20-26, wherein the inert carrier is dextrose monohydrate. 28. The composition of any one of claims 20-27, wherein the composition has a moisture content of less than about 5%; and a final bacterial concentration of about 10 5 - 10 11 colony forming units (CFU) per gram of the composition. 29. The composition of any one of claims 20-28, wherein the inert carrier is at a concentration of about between 75 - 99.9% (w/w).
